top_signals.title: Strive CIO says prolonged bitcoin weakness could drive treasury firm consolidation
top_signals.summary: Ben Werkman said prolonged bitcoin weakness could increase pressure on treasury companies that relied heavily on convertible debt financing.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic to return to normal as soon as August, Kalshi traders speculate
top_signals.summary: Odds that the Strait of Hormuz traffic will return to normal before August surpassed 50%, following U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Routing guides are crumbling: ‘It is different this time’
top_signals.summary:
Some shippers are being forced to reprice their entire book as contractual truckload rates set just a couple of months ago are no longer being honored.

The post Routing guides are crumbling: ‘It is different this time’ appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Largest wind farm in the United States slated to begin commercial operations
top_signals.summary: The SunZia Wind Project, the largest wind farm in the United States, is slated to begin commercial operations this month. The wind farm, located in New Mexico, has a total net summer generating capacity of 3,650 megawatts (MW) and is composed of 916 wind turbines. SunZia's capacity is more than three times larger than the next two largest wind farms, Alta Wind in Southern California (1,098 MW) and Great Prairie in northern Texas (1,027 MW). The SunZia Wind Project works with a high voltage transmission line to deliver the wind power generated to Arizona and California.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Royal Caribbean Begins Construction on Seventh Oasis Class Vessel
top_signals.summary: Royal Caribbean has celebrated the keel-laying of its seventh Oasis Class vessel at the Chantiers de l'Atlantique shipyard in Saint-Nazaire, France. The soon-to-be-named ship will join the revolutionary Oasis Class lineup in 2028. The vessel follows Oasis of the Seas (2009)…
